description:
Offered: every other year
This seminar course examines the social construction of gender and gender inequality at the individual, interactional, and macro levels of society. It employs an intersectional approach that considers gender in relation to race, social class, sexual identity, and other social factors.
Prerequisite(s): SOC 1100 and sophomore, junior or senior standing
Meets General Education Requirement(s): CV, DP
prerequisites: SOC 1100 and sophomore, junior or senior standing
